title: The merge role: merge a finished worktree, close its pane, and stop dead on a red verify
action: Add the merge role, mirroring the dispatch role's structure. It must: self-name its pane `merge` (D17); find finished worktrees using ONLY bee's own record - phase compounding-complete, zero cells open or claimed, clean tree, on the expected wt/<slug> branch (D2, D20) - and never read herdr's agent_status as proof of completion (D18); run `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs worktree merge --id <id> --cleanup` from the MAIN checkout; then close that worktree's runtime pane with `herdr pane close <pane_id>`, found by the pane whose label equals the worktree name (D18). On MERGE_VERIFY_RED it STOPS: no retry of the verify, no merge, no cleanup - one report into the chat pane naming the worktree and the failure, then the loop continues to the next cycle (D3, D19). Retrying would be worse than the false alarm it dodges: a genuine semantic conflict that happens to pass on a second run would slip through the only gate the merge has. Also write references/merge-prompt.md mirroring the dispatch prompt, and remove control-loop.sh's `--role merge is not implemented yet` guard now that the prompt exists. All copies byte-identical.

truths: The merge role decides a worktree is finished from bee state alone, never from agent_status
A red verify produces one report and no merge, no cleanup, and no retry
The runtime pane is closed only after a successful merge, and is located by its label
merge-prompt.md exists and mirrors the dispatch prompt's shape
Every file has a byte-identical copy in the other skill root

artifacts.substantive: a merge role section with the four finished-conditions and the red-verify stop
the per-cycle prompt routing a cold agent into the merge role

prohibitions: Do not add any retry, backoff, or second attempt around a red verify (D3)
Do not let the merge role reclaim or close a pane on anything but a successful merge
Do not read herdr agent_status as evidence an item is finished (D20)

verify: set -e; A=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/SKILL.md; M=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/merge-prompt.md; S=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; diff -q "$A" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/SKILL.md; diff -q "$M" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/merge-prompt.md; diff -q "$S" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; test -s "$M"; grep -qi 'herdr-orchestrating' "$M"; grep -q 'worktree merge' "$A"; grep -q -- '--cleanup' "$A"; grep -q 'MERGE_VERIFY_RED' "$A"; grep -q 'pane close' "$A"; for d in D2 D3 D18 D20; do grep -qE "\b$d\b" "$A" || { echo "missing $d"; exit 1; }; done; ! grep -qi 'is not implemented yet' "$S"; mkdir -p .bee/tmp/stubbin2; printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nprintf "STUB_MERGE_ROLE\\n"\n' > .bee/tmp/stubbin2/claude; chmod +x .bee/tmp/stubbin2/claude; OUT=$(PATH="$PWD/.bee/tmp/stubbin2:$PATH" bash "$S" --role merge --max-iterations 1 --interval 1 2>&1); echo "$OUT" | grep -q 'STUB_MERGE_ROLE'; rm -rf .bee/tmp/stubbin2; echo MERGE_ROLE_WIRED

trace.outcome: The merge role: finished worktrees decided by bee alone, merged and cleaned up, their pane closed - and a red verify that stops for good, not just until the next poll.
trace.verify_command: set -e; A=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/SKILL.md; M=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/merge-prompt.md; S=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; diff -q "$A" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/SKILL.md; diff -q "$M" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/merge-prompt.md; diff -q "$S" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; test -s "$M"; grep -qi 'herdr-orchestrating' "$M"; grep -q 'worktree merge' "$A"; grep -q -- '--cleanup' "$A"; grep -q 'MERGE_VERIFY_RED' "$A"; grep -q 'pane close' "$A"; for d in D2 D3 D18 D20; do grep -qE "\b$d\b" "$A" || { echo "missing $d"; exit 1; }; done; ! grep -qi 'is not implemented yet' "$S"; mkdir -p .bee/tmp/stubbin2; printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nprintf "STUB_MERGE_ROLE\\n"\n' > .bee/tmp/stubbin2/claude; chmod +x .bee/tmp/stubbin2/claude; OUT=$(PATH="$PWD/.bee/tmp/stubbin2:$PATH" bash "$S" --role merge --max-iterations 1 --interval 1 2>&1); echo "$OUT" | grep -q 'STUB_MERGE_ROLE'; rm -rf .bee/tmp/stubbin2; echo MERGE_ROLE_WIRED
